name: Humitas
recipeIngredient: 8 Ears of fresh corn (with shucks on)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the kernels off the corn cobs and save the cobs and husks.
2. Place the largest husks in a pan of boiling water to soften.
3. In a separate pan, melt the butter over medium heat.
4. Add the chopped onion and cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
5. Add the tomato, salt, pepper, and sugar; stir well. Cook for an additional 5-6 minutes.
6. Remove the softened husks from the water and pat dry with a paper towel.
7. Spread approximately 3 tablespoons of the corn mixture on the center of each husk.
8. Fold the sides of the husk over to form a package, tucking them under the edges to create a tight seal (use two leaves if necessary).
9. Tie securely with string or kitchen twine.
10. Place the packaged humitas in the bottom of a large pot and cover with boiling water.
11. Cook for 30 minutes.
12. Serve hot, optionally adding garlic or a splash of Sriracha or Tuong hot sauce to taste.
